name: Documentation site OIDC section, upgrade guide & public-API consolidation
outcome: The OIDC layer's per-feature docs (added in issues 4, 7, 8) are stitched together into a coherent OIDC section, the upgrade guide covers the verify() pinning and at+jwt behavior changes, and the public-API surface is audited end-to-end. Per-issue export confirmation already happened in issues 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8.
scope: Docs-site integration, the upgrade guide, public-API audit, and final consumer-facing artifacts. Per-issue export and per-endpoint documentation already shipped with their feature issues (MF-8 distributes docs). This issue is the final integration pass — re-pointed 2 → 3 to reflect actual scope. The example app persistence update is handled by issue 10; cross-cutting resilience/conformance validation is handled by issue 11.
acceptance_criteria: Public-API audit: a single test or build script asserts every new OIDC public type and the AccessTokenVerifier are importable from the package entry point (catches anything the feature issues missed)
CLAUDE.md describes the OIDC layer in the architecture section, including the AccessTokenVerifier seam's role

Docs site has a coherent OIDC section assembled from the per-feature pages shipped in issues 4, 7, and 8, plus three new integration pages added in this issue: (a) Getting Started with OIDC, (b) Keypair Lifecycle, (c) Hooks reference

Keypair Lifecycle page includes openssl and Node generateKeyPairSync snippets, PEM vs KeyObject usage, a do-not-generate-on-boot warning, and a forward-pointer to multi-key rotation as a future change

Configuration page (configuration.md) gains a complete options table for the oidc config block (every field, every type, every default) and the AuthorizationServerOptions diff (top-level issuer + nested oidc — issue 2's note on why the split exists)

Hooks reference includes a 'which hook do I use?' decision table comparing getUserClaims (scope-derived claims, UserInfo only) vs getIdTokenClaims (custom protocol-adjacent claims in the ID token); TSDoc doc-comments are required on the exported callback types and asserted by lint or build

Upgrade guide (upgrade_guide.md) covers (a) the verify() algorithm pin — caller-supplied algorithms VerifyOptions is now ignored; (b) the typ:at+jwt JOSE header on OIDC access tokens; (c) the opaque-code nonce/auth_time persistence requirement; (d) the OAuthAuthCode entity diff; (e) the BearerTokenResponse.body.id_token shape extension
CHANGELOG: stitched from the per-issue CHANGELOG entries added by issues 1 and 4, plus the OIDC feature summary
Known limitations list: no ID token on refresh; no offline_access auto-recognition (consumer-owned); no ES256 in v1 (forward-pointer to multi-key story); no signed/encrypted UserInfo

Opaque-code nonce/auth_time obligation is documented as a prominent callout, alongside a recommendation to use JWT auth codes for OIDC (issue 3's fail-loud guard backs the obligation, but the doc still warns consumers building opaque-code repositories)
openid-client smoke test recipe: a copy-pasteable snippet that confirms the OIDC integration works against the popular openid-client library — release gate
steps: index.ts — audit and confirm exports for: OIDC option types, oidc config block type, ID token claim types, discovery types, getUserClaims/getIdTokenClaims callback types, AccessTokenVerifier, OAuthException.invalidToken/insufficientScope helpers, PROTOCOL_CLAIM_NAMES const
CLAUDE.md — add an OIDC section to the architecture description, including a one-paragraph description of the AccessTokenVerifier seam and why it exists
docs site — write the Getting Started with OIDC page (config block → key setup → enable → call the new endpoints)
docs site — write the Keypair Lifecycle page with the snippets and warnings listed in the acceptance criteria
docs site — write the Hooks reference with the 'which hook do I use?' decision table
docs site configuration page — add the oidc config block options table and the top-level-issuer / nested-oidc split rationale
docs site — verify the page builds, links from the navigation, and that the per-feature pages from issues 4, 7, 8 are linked from the OIDC section
upgrade_guide.md — add the five upgrade items in the acceptance criteria; cross-link to the CHANGELOG entries from issues 1 and 4
CHANGELOG — finalize the OIDC release summary, stitching in the per-issue entries
lint/build — assert TSDoc doc-comments are present on the exported callback types
notes: Final integration slice. The adapters need no code changes — the three OIDC endpoints return a plain ResponseInterface every adapter already handles, so the docs only show wiring. Per-feature docs ship with their issues (MF-8); this issue is integration, the upgrade guide, the public-API audit. The example app update moved to issue 10 and resilience/conformance validation moved to issue 11. ADRs 0001/0002/0003 and CONTEXT.md already capture the rationale and glossary.
